name: Wadjet
description: Wadjet, also known as Uraeus, is one of the oldest deities of Egypt. She was originally the local goddess of the city of Dep (later Buto), which became part of Lower Egypt. Wadjet was considered the protector of Lower Egypt, and later became the patron and protector of all of Egypt. She is often depicted as a cobra, specifically the Egyptian cobra, ready to strike, or as a woman with the head of a cobra. Her image adorns the crowns of the pharaohs, symbolizing her protection and divine authority. Wadjet is associated with royalty, protection, healing, and Lower Egypt. As a protector goddess, she defends against enemies and evil forces. She is also associated with the sun and fire, often depicted with solar imagery. Her power extends to healing, as she was believed to possess medicinal knowledge and the ability to cure snake bites. She is a powerful symbol of royal authority and divine protection, safeguarding the pharaohs and the land of Egypt from harm. Wadjet's vigilance and power were crucial for maintaining order and stability in the kingdom, making her one of the most revered and important deities in the Egyptian pantheon. Her role as a protector and symbol of power ensured her enduring significance throughout Egyptian history.
subtitle: Protector of Lower Egypt and Pharaohs

keyMyths.title: Wadjet's Protection of Horus
keyMyths.description: Wadjet protected the infant Horus from Set in the marshes of the Delta after Osiris' death. She hid him and nurtured him until he was old enough to avenge his father.

keyMyths.title: Wadjet and the Pharaoh's Crown
keyMyths.description: Wadjet became a symbol of royal authority and protection when she was placed on the pharaoh's crown as the Uraeus. Her presence ensured the pharaoh's safety and divine right to rule.

keyMyths.title: Wadjet as a Fire Goddess
keyMyths.description: In some myths, Wadjet is associated with fire and the sun, representing her ability to destroy enemies and protect against evil. Her fiery gaze could ward off any threat to the pharaoh and Egypt.

sources.author: Richard H. Wilkinson
sources.work: Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt
sources.description: Detailed descriptions of Egyptian deities and their roles.
sources.text: Detailed descriptions of Egyptian deities and their roles.
